description_long: This object consists of three distinct grave markers, each crafted from a speckled, dark stone resembling granite. The first marker is a tall, rectangular cross mounted on a stepped base, with a classic Latin cross shape and a rough, weathered texture. The second marker is a rounded headstone with a simple arched top and a flat, unadorned face, suggesting a traditional gravestone design. The third marker is a rectangular monument with a slightly ornate, tiered top and a broad, flat face, likely intended for inscriptions or epitaphs. All three markers display a mottled pattern of gray and yellowish specks, giving them an aged, mossy appearance. The surfaces are hard and rough, typical of outdoor stone memorials, and the forms are solid and heavy, indicating permanence. These grave markers are commonly found in urban cemeteries or graveyards, serving as memorials for the deceased.
description: A set of three heavy, speckled granite grave markers: a Latin cross, a rounded headstone, and a rectangular monument, all with rough, weathered surfaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three grave markers: the cross on the left, the rounded headstone in the center, and the rectangular monument on the right.
description_view_1: Side view focusing on the narrow profile of the cross, with the other markers mostly obscured.
description_view_2: Rear view of the grave markers, showing the backs of the rectangular monument and rounded headstone, with the cross on the right.
description_view_3: Opposite side view of the cross, showing its narrow profile and the backs of the other two markers.
